summary: Iteration 4 backend testing complete. Refactor verified (server.py + deps.py + models.py + 5 routers) — all 54 prior regression tests still pass (17 rated_worktops + 19 iter2 + 18 iter3) with zero modifications. Added 13 NEW iteration4 tests covering: /api/health still 200 from server.py; new viz has published=true in BOTH response and Mongo doc; PATCH /visualizations/{id} happy path (200 + returned doc has published=False), other-user (404), bogus-id (404), empty body (400), unauthenticated (401); per-stone showroom EXCLUDES unpublished viz and re-INCLUDES it after republish; showroom respects the 8-render cap and returns [] for a brand-new stone with no renders. ONE real Gemini call total (reused across the iter4 module). All routers are correctly mounted under /api in the documented order (auth, stones, visualize, quotes, credits). No URL or response contracts changed.

minor.issue: 404 used to denote both 'not found' AND 'not your viz' (intentional — avoids leaking existence to other users). Documented behavior, not a bug, but worth noting in API docs.

`renders` array still embeds `result_image` URL strings. Iteration 3 noted this — fine now that all new docs use object-storage URLs (~50 byte strings), but legacy data-URL docs (if any remain on this stone) would still bloat the payload. Cosmetic.

critical_code_review_comments: Refactor is clean: server.py now ~115 lines (was 844). deps.py centralizes DB + JWT + bcrypt + get_current_user/require_admin. models.py has the new VisualizationUpdate model. Each router is self-contained.

routers/visualize.py imports `fetch_stone_payload` from routers/stones — server.py mounts stones BEFORE visualize, so import ordering at app boot is fine; the cross-router function-import couples the two but is acceptable for now.

PATCH endpoint correctly uses `find_one_and_update` with both `id` and `user_id` in the filter — single round-trip, secure (no separate 'fetch then check owner' race), and naturally returns 404 for both 'not found' and 'not yours'. Good.
VisualizationUpdate.published defaults to None and is excluded via model_dump(exclude_none=True), so PATCH with `{}` produces an empty update dict and the endpoint correctly raises 400. Verified.

Public showroom filter uses `published: {$ne: False}` — this correctly INCLUDES legacy docs that pre-date the `published` field (where the key is missing). Good design choice for backward compatibility.
visualize.py still has the inline base64 fallback if object storage fails — same warning as iter3 (could grow doc to ~700KB). Not a blocker.
Minor: empty-body PATCH returns 400 with detail 'No fields to update' — consistent with admin_update_stone in stones.py. Good consistency.

Minor: PATCH endpoint does not enforce a body shape — sending `{published: 'yes'}` (string) is rejected by Pydantic as 422; sending `{foo: 'bar'}` returns 400 because no known fields are set. Both acceptable.
test_credentials: Admin admin@ratedworktops.com / Admin@RW2026 verified. Fresh users registered per run (it4owner_*, it4other_*).
seed_data_creation: Per iter4 run: 2 fresh users (owner + other), 1 real Gemini viz (uploaded to object storage), 1 temporary house stone (created + deleted within test_showroom_empty_safe). No mutations to seeded admin or featured flags.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 4 = refactor + tiny `published` feature, all green. The full regression matrix (rated_worktops + iter2 + iter3 + iter4 = 67 tests) runs in ~85s with 2-3 Gemini calls total. To minimize Gemini spend during quick checks, run iter4 alone (1 call, 13 tests). Note the cross-router import in visualize.py (`from routers.stones import fetch_stone_payload`) — if future iterations re-order router registration in server.py, ensure stones is mounted before visualize. PATCH /visualizations/{id} currently only supports the `published` field; extending the model is trivial via VisualizationUpdate in models.py.
rca of the issue: No bugs to RCA — all tests pass first try.
